title: Malva parnassiifolia
taxonomy: Malva parnassiifolia is classified in the family Malvaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as mallow or hibiscus family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Malva parnassiifolia belonging to the subfamily Malvoideae. Malvaceae is a member of the order Malvales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the order Celastrales and the family Celastraceae. The order Malvales is part of the clade Rosids, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Malpighiales and Celastrales. Malva parnassiifolia's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Malva parnassiifolia is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Malva parnassiifolia is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective against certain infections. Additionally, it is used to soothe sore throats and coughs due to its mucilaginous properties.
benefits: Malva parnassiifolia is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidant effects. properties: Malva parnassiifolia, also known as musk mallow, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating minor wounds and skin infections. constituents: Malva parnassiifolia contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. parts: Malva parnassiifolia, also known as Mallow, is a plant with a long history of medicinal use. The leaves, flowers, and roots of the plant are commonly used in herbal medicine.
